head: Data Specialist - Data & Analytics - Beijing(315212)
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 1 周前 · 29 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Pursue a Career with Impact

At Deloitte China, we are on a mission to nurture and empower our people to become deep subject matter experts. We offer the perfect platform to unleash your full potential and equip you to thrive on challenges, and partner with our clients to solve their most complex problems. A world of opportunities awaits. Start your adventure and journey with us.
About The Business

Deloitte brings world-class capabilities and high-quality service to clients, delivering the insights they need to address their most complex business challenges. In the digital era, technology innovation and business are inextricably connected to each other. Given the high demand of technology-enabled business, Deloitte professionals help our clients facing the most critical information and technology challenges in their business.

We provide a broad spectrum of services to help our clients make greater impact on finance strategy formulation and execution. Our practice leverages resources, skills and technical tools from our various disciplines to help executives fulfil their evolving, expanded and increasingly complex roles, particularly in the areas of complex finance and accounting, as well as the development and implementation of strategies, to facilitate greater efficiencies and enable increased profitability.
Work you'll do

Conduct in-depth qualitative and quantitative analyses of clients’ financial, operational, and user data by leveraging statistical methods and big data techniques. Identify key risk indicators (KRIs) and support audit teams in performing risk assessment procedures

Check data integrity by validating computational accuracy and completeness in both operational and financial data, while detecting diverse risks across internal and external data sources through multidimensional data verification processes

Independently prepare audit workpapers and reports aligned with Deloitte standards. Actively contribute to core tasks throughout the project lifecycle, including summarizing interview notes, collecting documentation, creating process flow diagrams, and executing multi-dimensional data analysis

Proactively identify and explore innovative applications of cutting-edge technologies within audit workflows. Provide valuable insights and professional support to technology innovation teams, driving advancements in intelligent auditing capabilities and fostering continuous improvement in audit processes
Produce a high-quality work products and collaborate with others across phases of the project lifecycle to deliver a superior client experience
Manage work in an ethical manner with continuous attention to quality standards and risk rules, and maintaining confidentiality

During your tenure with us, you will demonstrate and develop your leadership and professional capabilities in the following areas: Inspiring, Creating purpose, Driving agility, Building diverse capability, Influencing, Collaborating, Delivering value, Building the business, Analytical acumen, Effective communication, Engagement management/delivery excellence, Managing change, Managing quality & risk, Strategic thinking and problem solving, and Tech savviness.
We are looking for someone with
To be considered for this position, you must demonstrate skills and experience in the following areas:
Bachelor or equivalent degree in statistics, mathematics, computer science, or a related area
At least 3 years of experience in finance, accounting, external audit, data analysis or a related field (including finance systems)
Demonstrates understanding of their role in planning the audit, performing tests of controls, and/or performing substantive procedures
Leverages technology to capitalize on the power of analytics and choose the appropriate technique for the underlying data set
Well-developed relationship management skills including teamwork and collaboration
Understands the limitations in analysis software and required input data format and is able to tweak the dataset to derive an effective visual
Fluent in at least 1-2 programming languages
Proficient in SQL, Python, R, Microsoft Power BI, Word, Excel and PowerPoint etc.

Deloitte has been named China's Top Employer since 2006, Universum's Most Attractive Employer in China since 2008, and the Best Workplaces in Greater China since 2019.

head: Consultant and Associate, Experienced Hire, Full-time, Greater China
info: 南京地区 · 4 天前 · 37 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Locations
: Shanghai | Beijing | Shenzhen | Hong Kong | Taipei
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.
What You'll Do

As a member of our consulting team, you’ll work alongside some of the world’s top minds on cases that reshape business, government, and society. You’ll collaborate on challenging projects with team members from many backgrounds and disciplines, increasing your understanding of complex business problems from diverse perspectives and developing new skills and experience to help you at every stage of your career – at BCG and beyond.

Consulting work is varied and rigorous, much of it performed at our client sites. Projects can vary in length, size, and location, depending on the client’s challenge. Because our clients operate all over the world, you may travel internationally.

If you are joining us directly from school or with a few years of experience, expect to spend time working across a wide range of clients and projects from helping to devise the integration strategy for a pharmaceutical merger to developing sustainable farming practices in emerging countries.

For more experienced professionals, or as your career advances, you’ll begin to specialize in one or more practice areas, perhaps as part of our expert consulting track for those with deep knowledge and skills in a particular discipline or industry. Every step of the way you will be guided by BCG mentors and world-class learning programs designed to meet your individual needs.
What You'll Bring
Strategic thinking and strong analytical skills
A hypothesis-driven problem solving approach
Good numerical skills
Team spirit, open and positive in a group dynamic
Excellent communication skills
Business proficiency in both Chinese and English

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
BCG is an E - Verify Employer.

company_box_content: Randstad is a global talent leader with the vision to be the world’s most equitable and specialised talent company. As a partner for talent and through our four specialisations - Operational, Professional, Digital and Enterprise - we provide clients with the high-quality, diverse and agile workforces that they need to succeed in a talent scarce world. We help people secure meaningful roles, develop relevant skills and find purpose and belonging in their workplace. Through the value we create, we are committed to a better and more sustainable future for all.

Headquartered in the Netherlands, Randstad operates in 39 markets and has 41,400 employees. In 2006, Randstad set sail for China, with our headquarters in Shanghai and offices in major cities such as Beijing, Nanjing, Suzhou, Guangzhou, Shenzhen and Hong Kong SAR. Randstad Greater China provides a comprehensive range of recruitment services and human resources solutions including: specialist permanent recruitment, executive search, staffing &amp
outsourcing, recruitment process outsourcing (RPO), managed services programs (MSP), outplacement, and talent mobility.
作为专业的综合人力资源服务机构，任仕达致力于成为全球最公平、最专业的人才公司。通过四大专业领域（Operational, Professional, Digital and Enterprise），我们一方面为客户赢得高质量、多元化与灵活的人才，助力企业成功；另一方面也为人才匹配有意义的工作机会，促进发展与提升职业技能，找到目标与归属感。通过价值创造，我们期待共创更美好的未来，可持续发展。

Bybit 致力于打造一个安全可靠、公开透明的交易平台，让投资者能够轻松探索充满魅力的加密货币世界。无论是进行现货或衍生品交易，探索挖矿和质押产品，还是利用 API 进行算法交易，Bybit 都能满足您的需求，让您掌控自己的加密货币之旅。
作为迅速发展的 Web3 赛道的先行者，Bybit 推出和孵化了一系列创新型加密货币项目、去中心化解决方案和数据驱动型研究，为加密货币的普及和发展搭建了一个繁荣的生态体系。我们始终秉承换位思考、快速响应和持续改进的理念，这些价值观已渗透到我们运营的方方面面，创造了一个极具包容性的环境，使每个人都能得到充分的关注和赋能。
此外，Bybit 还积极与世界各地的监管机构合作，在构建强大的安全保障框架方面扮演了关键角色，为加密货币的稳健快速发展奠定了基石。
Bybit 志在成为您在加密货币之旅上的可靠伙伴，与您一起拥抱数字变革！
#TheCryptoArk #Bybit
展开
company_description: A leading cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2018, serving millions of users globally with services in trading, mining, and Web3 innovations.

head: GTM
info: 中国 广东省 深圳 · 2 天前 · 21 位申请者由招聘者推广 · 积极审核申请者
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Job Responsibilities:

1. IMS Product Management: Responsible for the planning and commercial success of OPPO’s mid-to-high-end products in India, integrating and aligning product, marketing, and sales resources within the region, establishing and managing the IMS team, and developing and executing overall IMS strategies for the project.
2. Volume and Price Management: Maximize product returns (sales volume/profit/revenue) through effective volume and price management, ensuring the product's commercial success.

3. Deep Collaboration with Product Line: Conduct multidimensional in-depth insights into online and offline markets in India, participate in product definition, organize selling point planning, and develop competitive products based on customer and user needs.

4. Deep Collaboration with Marketing: Drive product awareness enhancement, participate in the output of key integrated marketing deliverables, and maintain product heat during launch and throughout its lifecycle.
Job Requirements:
1. Bachelor’s degree or higher, with 7+ years of experience in the mobile phone industry.
2. Work experience in at least one of the following areas: product planning, channel sales, e-commerce operations, retail management, or integrated marketing.
3. Strong market analysis and user insight capabilities, excellent strategic vision and planning skills.
4. Results-driven, highly motivated, strong execution ability, skilled in multi-project management, resource coordination, communication, and driving initiatives forward.
5. Fluent in English, willing to travel overseas for business.
company_box_content: Founded in 2004, OPPO is one of the world's leading innovators of smart devices. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, OPPO's more than 290,000 points of sales and 1,900 official service centers share the beauty of technology with users all over the world. To forward our vision of a better future, OPPO has established ten smart manufacturing facilities around the world, as well as a global design center in London. Every day, our 40,000 employees put their heart and soul into exploring the possibilities of humanistic technology.

OPPO is not just a device maker. We are a technology company that combines hardware with software and services. Our software range, built around the ColorOS operating system, makes devices more user-friendly and intelligent for our 500 million users worldwide. The OPPO App Market, OPPO Cloud also deliver smarter, more convenient, and better-connected services.

Moving forward, OPPO will remain committed to our mission of "Technology for Mankind, Kindness for the World." We will continue to pursue innovation in the smart themes of learning, productivity, entertainment, and healthcare to meet the needs of people around the world. Constantly seeking technological breakthroughs.
Make your moment with OPPO.
展开
company_description: A global smart device innovator with operations in over 60 countries, 290,000+ points of sale, and 40,000 employees, focusing on mobile phones and technology.

head: Data Engineer
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 1 个月前 · 86 位申请者由招聘者推广 · 尚无可用回复洞察
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
About the Role

We're looking for a Data Engineer to design and maintain data platforms that power trading and research. You'll work on data pipelines, APIs, storage systems, and quality monitoring to ensure reliable, scalable, and efficient data services.
Responsibilities
1. Design, develop, and maintain market data systems, APIs, and ETL pipelines.
2. Manage data assets including databases, cloud storage, and metadata.
3. Integrate data vendors and automate ingestion, migration, and workflows.
4. Implement monitoring and alerting for data quality and reliability.
5. Optimize platform performance and support analytics and research tools.
Qualifications
1. Bachelor's degree or above in Computer Science or related field.
2. Strong programming skills in Python (NumPy, Pandas) and solid SQL knowledge.
3. Familiarity with Linux/Unix and database principles.
4. Good understanding of algorithms, debugging, and system design.
5. Strong communication, problem-solving, and teamwork skills.
Required Skills
1. Strong programming skills in Python (NumPy, Pandas) and solid SQL knowledge.
2. Familiarity with Linux/Unix and database principles.
3. Good understanding of algorithms, debugging, and system design.
4. Strong communication, problem-solving, and teamwork skills.
Preferred Skills
1. Experience with Airflow, cloud storage, big data platforms, or data warehouses.
2. Knowledge of time-series databases (InfluxDB, TDengine) and distributed systems.
3. Additional back-end languages (C++/Go/Java) are a plus.
4. Background in finance or quantitative workflows is an advantage.

head: Editorial Data Analyst
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 重新发布时间: 2 周前 · 63 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Job Description
Editorial Data Analyst
Location:
Beijing, CHN

Our mission is to unlock human potential. We welcome you for who you are, the background you bring, and we embrace individuals who get excited about learning. Bring your experiences, your perspectives, and your passion
it’s in our differences that we empower the way the world learns.
About The Role
Position Overview

We are seeking a highly motivated Editorial Data Analyst to join our Business Analytics & Enablement team at Wiley. This role offers an exciting opportunity to work in a collaborative environment, leveraging data analytics and business intelligence tools to influence the performance of our products, platforms, and services within Research Publishing. The ideal candidate will be passionate about data discovery, possess strong analytical and communication skills, and enjoy using data to inform key decisions and drive continuous improvement across editorial operations.
Key Responsibilities
Performance Reporting and Analysis
Generate and review product, service, and platform performance reports and dashboards, sharing insights with key stakeholders.
Support and maintain Research Publishing's specific data tools, ensuring alignment across editorial models.
Monitor and assess platform and product performance, providing analytical support to drive improvements and meet KPIs.
Present data insights to stakeholders in a clear and actionable format.
Data Insights and Stakeholder Collaboration
Analyze performance trends across journals, services, and platforms, with a focus on both routine operations and periods of heightened attention (e.g., following releases).
Collaborate with internal teams such as Peer Review Performance Managers, Editorial Vendor Managers, and in-house editors to improve product/service performance.
Data Integrity Management
Ensure data integrity by troubleshooting issues and collaborating with stakeholders to provide solutions for data validity concerns.
Document activities in line with departmental frameworks, ensuring reproducibility and discoverability.
Decision-Making and Data Communication
Evaluate the significance of data insights and decide when to take action or communicate findings.
Identify key stakeholders who need to be informed of specific analyses and provide appropriate recommendations.
Offer analytical support for maintaining, optimizing, or sunsetting products or services.
Required Qualifications
Education:
Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field.
Experience:
1-3 years of relevant or transferable experience in data analytics or a related field.
Experience with data analytics methodologies, including data cleaning, exploration, and visualization.
Technical Skills:
Proficiency in SQL (Intermediate to Advanced level).
Experience with BI dashboard development (Intermediate to Advanced level), ideally in PowerBI.
Strong expertise in Microsoft Excel and data analysis.
Experience in using statistical tools like SPSS, SAS, STATA, or programming languages like Python or R for data analysis.
Familiarity with Power Automate is a plus.
Core Competencies:
Strong analytical and communication skills with a passion for data.
Ability to collaborate across teams and gain trust from various stakeholders.
Results-oriented, self-motivated, and able to work independently to achieve objectives.
Capable of troubleshooting and solving problems with attention to detail.
Experience in data storytelling and presenting complex information clearly.
Desirable Skills & Experience
Exposure to the scholarly publishing industry is preferred, but not required.
Previous experience in data integrity management or data visualization will be an advantage.
A proactive approach to learning new skills and methodologies in data analytics.
About Wiley

Wiley is a trusted leader in research and learning, our pioneering solutions and services are paving the way for knowledge seekers as they work to solve the world's most important challenges. We are advocates of advancement, empowering knowledge-seekers to transform today's biggest obstacles into tomorrow's brightest opportunities.

With over 200 years of experience in publishing, we continue to evolve knowledge seekers' steps into strides, illuminating their path forward to personal, educational, and professional success at every stage. Around the globe, we break down barriers for innovators, empowering them to advance discoveries in their fields, adapt their workforces, and shape minds.

Wiley is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. We evaluate all qualified applicants and treat all qualified applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, genetic information, or based on any individual's status in any group or class protected by applicable federal, state or local laws. Wiley is also committed to providing reasonable accommodation to applicants and employees with disabilities. Applicants who require accommodation to participate in the job application process may contact tasupport@wiley.com for assistance.

We are proud that our workplace promotes continual learning and internal mobility. Our values support courageous teammates, needle movers, and learning champions all while striving to support the health and well-being of all employees. We offer meeting-free Friday afternoons allowing more time for heads down work and professional development, and through a robust body of employee programing we facilitate a wide range of opportunities to foster community, learn, and grow.

We are committed to fair, transparent pay, and we strive to provide competitive compensation in addition to a comprehensive benefits package. It is anticipated that most qualified candidates will fall within the range, however the ultimate salary offered for this role may be higher or lower and will be set based on a variety of non-discriminatory factors, including but not limited to, geographic location, skills, and competencies. Wiley proactively displays target base pay range for United Kingdom, Canada and USA based roles.
When applying, please attach your resume/CV to be considered.

3. Support and maintain Research Publishing's specific data tools, ensuring alignment across editorial models.
4. Monitor and assess platform and product performance, providing analytical support to drive improvements and meet KPIs.
5. Present data insights to stakeholders in a clear and actionable format.

8. Experience with BI dashboard development (Intermediate to Advanced level), ideally in PowerBI.
9. Strong expertise in Microsoft Excel and data analysis.

Through access to trusted research, data, and  AI-powered platforms that power progress, we accelerate scientific breakthroughs, support learning, and drive innovation that redefine fields and improve lives. We're your partner in pioneering the future – in a world driven by curiosity and continuous discovery.

head: Marketing Science Specialist
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 重新发布时间: 2 周前 · 57 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Snap Inc

is a technology company. We believe the camera presents the greatest opportunity to improve the way people live and communicate. Snap contributes to human progress by empowering people to express themselves, live in the moment, learn about the world, and have fun together. The Company’s three core products are
Snapchat
, a visual messaging app that enhances your relationships with friends, family, and the world
Lens Studio
, an augmented reality platform that powers AR across Snapchat and other services
and its AR glasses,
Spectacles

We’re looking for a Marketing Science Specialist for E-commerce based in China to join our Measurement Team at Snap Inc. We are a team of scientists and researchers who work in a highly collaborative environment, using our suite of tools and ad products to improve the performance of Snap's advertising platform and drive increased value for our customers.
What you’ll do:
As an individual contributor, you will be responsible to provide strategic measurement advice to internal and external stakeholders while being hands on on measurement study set up and execution
Partner with the China Sales team to develop and execute the market growth strategy along with quarterly goals rooted in sound consumer research, advertiser performance, and industry knowledge

Be the primary driver of performance improvement across our top advertisers leveraging a mix of learning-agenda strategies, experimental design, causal analytics, and ads efficacy solution testing in the China market
Lead measurement, research, and learning plans for key clients in support of their media objectives and the long-term growth of the partnership
Develop a measurement strategy, prioritization, and roadmap for ads measurement and insights products for the E-commerce vertical
Work closely with sales and marketing teams to identify, test, and scale new ad products, features and solutions that have a positive impact on advertiser value

Lead the scoping and development of custom multi-study meta analysis in partnership with Data Science and Product teams to understand the relative impact of different marketing strategies across digital platforms and media
Build measurement capability and advocacy across your vertical and with cross-functional partners
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:
Ability to structure and conduct analyses to generate insight and recommendations
Ability to influence senior leadership through presentation of clear and concise insights and recommendations
Understanding of measurement concepts, solutions, and underlying statistical fundamentals leveraged in the ads efficacy and measurement ecosystem
Subject matter expertise in at least one of the following areas and familiarity with both
experimental design (sig testing, power calculation, sample design) or econometrics modeling
Expertise with at least one of the advanced analytical tools (e.g. SQL, R, Python)
Access, analyze, interpret, and communicate ads performance insights using a wide range of standard data science tooling
Understanding of the market needs and ability to creatively apply measurement solutions and insights in a way that improves advertiser performance and the value-proposition of Snap
A strong understanding of the digital advertising and measurement ecosystem
Minimum Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or business field or equivalent years of experience
3-5 years of advanced analytics and measurement experience within a technology company, media agency, consulting firm, advertiser, or research company
Experience with ads measurement in user growth and acquisition or eCommerce
Preferred Qualifications:
Advanced degree in business, math, statistics, economics, engineering or a related quantitative field
Direct work experience with top advertisers, tech publishers within the China market
Experience building and influencing client or partner relationships
If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please don’t be shy and provide us some
information

"Default Together" Policy at Snap: At Snap Inc. we believe that being together in person helps us build our culture faster, reinforce our values, and serve our community, customers and partners better through dynamic collaboration. To reflect this, we practice a “default together” approach and expect our team members to work in an office 4+ days per week.

At Snap, we believe that having a team of diverse backgrounds and voices working together will enable us to create innovative products that improve the way people live and communicate. Snap is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and committed to providing employment opportunities regardless of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding, age, sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other protected classification, in accordance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. EOE, including disability/vets.
Our Benefits

: Snap Inc. is its own community, so we’ve got your back! We do our best to make sure you and your loved ones have everything you need to be happy and healthy, on your own terms. Our benefits are built around your needs and include paid parental leave, comprehensive medical coverage, emotional and mental health support programs, and compensation packages that let you share in Snap’s long-term success!
1. As an individual contributor, you will be responsible to provide strategic measurement advice to internal and external stakeholders while being hands on on measurement study set up and execution
2. Partner with the China Sales team to develop and execute the market growth strategy along with quarterly goals rooted in sound consumer research, advertiser performance, and industry knowledge

3. Be the primary driver of performance improvement across our top advertisers leveraging a mix of learning-agenda strategies, experimental design, causal analytics, and ads efficacy solution testing in the China market
4. Lead measurement, research, and learning plans for key clients in support of their media objectives and the long-term growth of the partnership
5. Develop a measurement strategy, prioritization, and roadmap for ads measurement and insights products for the E-commerce vertical
6. Work closely with sales and marketing teams to identify, test, and scale new ad products, features and solutions that have a positive impact on advertiser value

8. Build measurement capability and advocacy across your vertical and with cross-functional partners

2. Ability to influence senior leadership through presentation of clear and concise insights and recommendations
3. Understanding of measurement concepts, solutions, and underlying statistical fundamentals leveraged in the ads efficacy and measurement ecosystem
4. Subject matter expertise in at least one of the following areas and familiarity with both; experimental design (sig testing, power calculation, sample design) or econometrics modeling
5. Expertise with at least one of the advanced analytical tools (e.g. SQL, R, Python)
6. Access, analyze, interpret, and communicate ads performance insights using a wide range of standard data science tooling
7. Understanding of the market needs and ability to creatively apply measurement solutions and insights in a way that improves advertiser performance and the value-proposition of Snap
8. A strong understanding of the digital advertising and measurement ecosystem

3. Experience with ads measurement in user growth and acquisition or eCommerce

2. Ability to influence senior leadership through presentation of clear and concise insights and recommendations
3. Understanding of measurement concepts, solutions, and underlying statistical fundamentals leveraged in the ads efficacy and measurement ecosystem
4. Subject matter expertise in at least one of the following areas and familiarity with both; experimental design (sig testing, power calculation, sample design) or econometrics modeling
5. Expertise with at least one of the advanced analytical tools (e.g. SQL, R, Python)
6. Access, analyze, interpret, and communicate ads performance insights using a wide range of standard data science tooling
7. Understanding of the market needs and ability to creatively apply measurement solutions and insights in a way that improves advertiser performance and the value-proposition of Snap
8. A strong understanding of the digital advertising and measurement ecosystem
1. Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or business field or equivalent years of experience
2. 3-5 years of advanced analytics and measurement experience within a technology company, media agency, consulting firm, advertiser, or research company
3. Experience with ads measurement in user growth and acquisition or eCommerce
1. Advanced degree in business, math, statistics, economics, engineering or a related quantitative field
2. Direct work experience with top advertisers, tech publishers within the China market
3. Experience building and influencing client or partner relationships

head: myBUY Controls, Compliance & Governance Assistant Manager; 采购控制与合规助理经理
info: 中国 四川省 成都市 · 重新发布时间: 3 天前 · 22 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Overview

This role will play a pivotal role within the Control & Governance team of the procurement team and has a main objective to contribute to risk mitigation, policy implementation, and the overall effectiveness of our procurement governance framework for APAC Sector.
Responsibilities
Compliance & Policy Adherence

Monitor procurement activities to ensure strict compliance with internal policies, procedures, and relevant regulatory requirements. Conduct regular reviews of purchase orders to identify any deviations/non-compliant activities from the policy.

Continuously monitor changes in relevant global policies and regulations and perform impact analysis on the existing controls and implement the required changes to the controls upon alignment from controls team.
Ensure the myBuy Policies and guidelines are in line with P2P and Global Procurement policy and guidelines.
Collaborate with key stakeholders from control and procurement teams to develop, update, and communicate procurement controls matrix.
Prepare training materials to employees within the department to enhance awareness of compliance obligations.
Ensure that the GCS scope controls are executed as per the controls design matrix and ensure the same has been documented in the GCS portal on timely manner.

Act as a liaison between the procurement department and internal auditors. Collaborate during audits, providing necessary documentation, explanations, and implementing corrective actions in a timely manner.
Risk Assessment and Mitigation

Develop and implement risk mitigation strategies, working closely with procurement stakeholders and continuously monitor the effectiveness of these strategies and make adjustments as needed. (e.g.: Governance calls).
Support and review myBUY CR initiatives/system upgrade with subject matter experts (SMEs) in APAC.
Process Standardization
Closely work with myBUY myBUY control, compliance and governance GPO to drive standardization across all sectors.
Qualifications
Bachelor degree in Finance/Supply Chain/Management/Accounting/Business Administration. A master degree is a plus.
5+ Years of experience required.
Functional knowledge: Depth of different aspects of Procurement such as processes and tools, knowledge in Food & Beverage industry a plus.
Experience in sourcing processes and technologies, with experience of implementing them in a global and regional environment.
Advanced proficiency in data analysis tools, including Microsoft Excel (e.g., advanced formulas, pivot tables, data validation)
data visualization tools skills like Tableau or Power BI is a plus.
Strong communication & negotiation skills .
Results Oriented - comfortable setting and delivering against bold, stretch targets.
English essential, both verbal and in writing.
Comfortable working within matrix organization .
Affinity with technology applications and business processes and the impact on people.
Strong analytical, problem solving, and root cause identification skills.
Solid understanding of the PepsiCo organization and cultural differences.

1.  Monitor procurement activities to ensure strict compliance with internal policies, procedures, and relevant regulatory requirements. Conduct regular reviews of purchase orders to identify any deviations/non-compliant activities from the policy.

2.  Continuously monitor changes in relevant global policies and regulations and perform impact analysis on the existing controls and implement the required changes to the controls upon alignment from controls team.

company_box_content: PepsiCo is a playground for curious people. We invite thinkers, doers, and changemakers to champion innovation, take calculated risks, and challenge the status quo. From executives to team members on the front lines, we’re excited about the future. We take chances. Together, we dare to make the world a better place.

Our associates are the magic ingredient. Each of them plays an integral role in helping create deep connections between people and our products. Think about your last group celebration: Chances are, one of our iconic brands was by your side. At PepsiCo, you’re invited to be a part of a global team of innovators who make, move, and sell these products—which are enjoyed by more than 1 billion people a day.

A career at PepsiCo means working in a culture where everyone’s welcome. Here, you can dare to be yourself. No matter who you are or where you’re from, you can influence the people around you and the world at large. By showing up, you’ll have the opportunity to learn, develop and grow your skills for the future. Our supportive teams can fuel your professional goals to make a global impact on people and the planet.
Join us. Dare for Better.
展开
company_description: A global food and beverage company with iconic brands, focusing on innovation, sustainability, and a diverse workforce across multiple markets.

head: 数据科学家 Data Scientist（太仓）
info: 中国 江苏省 太仓市 · 重新发布时间: 1 周前 · 13 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介

Schaeffler is a dynamic global technology company and its success has been a result of its entrepreneurial spirit and long history of private ownership. Does that sound interesting to you? As a partner to all of the major automobile manufacturers, as well as key players in the aerospace and industrial sectors, we offer you many development opportunities.
Global Position Name
Data Scientist
Responsibilities

For this position, you will design and develop AI/ML solutions using a combination of traditional machine learning, deep learning, large language models (LLMs), generative AI, and intelligent agents to address various manufacturing challenges. Typical tasks include:
Developing predictive maintenance models using sensor data and time series forecasting techniques.
Implementing quality prediction and defect detection models leveraging computer vision and deep learning.
Applying large language models and generative AI for intelligent knowledge management, natural language understanding, and decision support.
Creating intelligent agents to optimize production scheduling and operational workflows.
Collaborating with plant engineers and domain experts to identify data-driven opportunities and validate AI models in real manufacturing settings.
Communication: You will visualize, explain, and present AI/ML results, from traditional models to advanced generative AI solutions, to ensure clear understanding by business stakeholders.
Implementation: You will work closely with software and automation teams to integrate AI solutions into manufacturing execution systems for real-time production support.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree or above in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Mechanical Engineering, or related disciplines.
5+ years of professional experience in AI/ML, including traditional machine learning, deep learning, and modern large model/LLM technologies
manufacturing domain experience is preferred.
Proficient in Python and familiar with machine learning libraries such as scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, as well as deep learning frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow.
Experience with large language models (e.g., GPT, BERT), generative AI frameworks, and transfer learning techniques.
Hands-on with typical manufacturing datasets: time series sensor signals, image/video data for vision tasks, text data for NLP applications.
Ability to design, train and deploy AI models that integrate multi-source heterogeneous data for manufacturing problem-solving.
Strong communication skills in English, both written and oral.
Proactive, collaborative, analytical mindset with good problem-solving skills and ability to work under pressure.

As a global company with employees around the world, it is important to us that we treat each other with respect and value all ideas and perspectives. By appreciating our differences, we inspire creativity and drive innovation. In this way, we contribute to sustainable value creation for our stakeholders and society as a whole. Together, we advance how the world moves.
Exciting assignments and outstanding development opportunities await you because we impact the future with innovation. We look forward to your application.

Yan Hong

head: BI Analyst (Supply Analytics, Bangkok-based, Relocation provided)
info: 中国 广东省 深圳 · 重新发布时间: 1 周前 · 6 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.
THIS ROLE IS BASED IN BANGKOK, THAILAND (WITH RELOCATION PROVIDED)
Location: Bangkok, Thailand (*Not open for remote work)
Get to Know our Team:

As part of the Supply Analytics, you will be responsible for identifying new partners and opportunities, cultivating a relationship with them, and working together with them to deliver the best outcome for our end customers. You would also be responsible with managing the day-to-day interaction with the partner and working closely with other teams within Agoda to ensure the partners needs are met.

We design tailored business and product solutions with our partners and help them generate measurable value. Members of our team are empowered and supported to grow their market(s) or accounts. We develop win-win relationships and leverage Agoda’s unique accommodations portfolio and tech solutions to bring our partners the advantages they seek. Utilizing our strong brand and resources, we build new channels to increase the visibility of Agoda, introduce more travelers to our great products and service and deliver significant revenues to the overall business.
The Opportunity:

As a BI Analyst, you will report directly to either the Senior Manager or Associate Director within the Supply Department and this will be an individual contributor role. You will be responsible and fully empowered to work with the partners on the ground. You will be supported by a team within the Supply department and work closely with other Team members within Agoda.
In this Role, you’ll get to
Translate internal briefs into analytical projects (to include refining the initial brief and asking the ‘right questions’, working through potential hypotheses and storyboarding the output)
Use and analyze data from multiple large-scale data warehouses and present statistically strong analysis to a wide range of business stakeholders.
Proactively identify opportunities for growth within supply and the wider business.
Drive new analytical initiatives and projects aimed at improving organizational efficiency and shaping Agoda supply.
Identify, support, and lead projects aimed at scaling up the way the Supply organization leverages on data, insights, and intelligence.
Automate manual operational processes and present back on time savings gained through modernization of business operations
What you’ll Need to Succeed
At least 2-5+ years of experience working as an Analyst with experience in analytics/data science/insights/strategy/BI
Advanced working knowledge and hands-on experience in SQL
Strong knowledge and hands-on experience in data visualization tools such as Tableau (preferably)
Expert domain of data analysis and data visualization tools and software such as Excel, Python (or R)
Bachelor’s degree ideally in a business or quantitative subject (e.g. computer science, mathematics, engineering, science, economics or finance).
A good understanding of statistical modelling knowledge or any machine learning technique knowledge (such as hypothesis testing, regression, logistic regression, random forest, etc.)
Good stakeholder management experience. Comfortable presenting to senior leadership and C-suite.
Experience in conducting A/B testing experimentation
Strong experience in finding data insights and provide business recommendation to the business

A hacker’s mindset – the ability to build simple but clever and elegant solutions to new problems within significant resource, operational and time constraints through deep understanding of the business, creative problem solving, and a wide range of expertise in data, analytics, automation, programming, and prototyping.
Excellent communicator with superior written, verbal, presentation and interpersonal communication skills.
Data driven in both decision making and performance measurement.
Extreme comfort in ambiguous, fast-paced environment.
Ability to multi-task, prioritize and coordinate resources.
It’s Great if you Have:
MBA or Masters in a quantitative subject (e.g. computer science, mathematics, engineering, science, economics or finance).
Program management certifications (e.g. PMI, PRINCE2) to compliment your program management experience.
Asian market experience.
Travel industry / e-commerce / tech / consulting experience.

head: Analyst, Procurement CoE

job_details_content: ##              职位简介
What You Will Do
Manage the supplier life cycle of supplier onboarding, from initial request to the vendor creation.
Collect and review proactively with new suppliers’ required documentation (e.g., W-9/W-8, certifications, compliance questionnaire updates.)
Review and validate submitted supplier information for completeness, accuracy, and adherence to company policies and regulatory requirements.
Coordinate with internal departments (e.g., Legal, Finance, Compliance) to ensure all necessary approvals and system updates are completed for new suppliers.
Execute regular updates, changes, and deactivations of existing supplier records in the relevant ERP/Procurement systems (e.g., SAP Ariba, Coupa,)
Perform periodic data cleansing and validation activities to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and eliminate redundancies.
Respond to internal and external inquiries related to supplier data, providing accurate and timely information.
Monitor and action requests for supplier data modifications received through various channels.
Identify opportunities to streamline and improve supplier onboarding and data maintenance processes for greater efficiency and accuracy.

Conduct training sessions (virtual and/or in-person as needed) for suppliers on how to effectively use the business network functionalities (e.g., invoice submission, order confirmation, catalog management, performance monitoring).
Develop and maintain(translation) comprehensive training materials, user guides and video tutorials for supplier enablement initiatives.
Track and report on business network-supplier enablement and adoption rates.
Function as the primary point of contact for supplier inquiries and technical support related to the supplier enablement and adoption initiatives.
Identify opportunities to enhance the supplier enablement experience and streamline related processes (e.g., onboarding workflows, communication methods)
Participate in system testing and upgrades related to supplier master data.
Bridge effectively with Procurement, Accounts Payable, Legal, and other internal teams to ensure smooth supplier operations.
Troubleshoot and resolve any issues or discrepancies during the onboarding process in a timely manner.

Extensive delivery through analyzing issues in a complex business process and preparing functional updates, user article knowledge, and reports.

head: FIN Analyst
info: 中国 重庆市 重庆市 · 重新发布时间: 4 天前 · 32 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Responsibilities

Budget formulation and rolling forecast: Develop the annual budget based on sales volume and industry competition predictions of the business. Monitor monthly forecasts based on the actual sales delivery and expenses continuously to identify risks and opportunities of the operation.

Policy Analysis and Budget Control: Woking with sales and marketing, analyze the policy to make sure the effectiveness, feasibility and controlled within budget and provide financial support and advice to business teams. Review business policy announcements to ensure consistency with approved policy and operability.

Marketing Expense Reimbursement: Review the sales volume submitted by dealers and the calculation of variable marketing expenses based on the announced marketing policy to ensure that the payment consistent with the proposal and announcement.

Variable Marketing Expense Closing: Conduct accounting of variable marketing expenses based on the actual sales volume and released policy. Compared with the monthly forecast, analyze the variance of business changes to optimize it continuously.

Standardization and Dealer Support: Regularly review and optimize the variable marketing expense process to make sure the standardized operations. Support the special reports of dealers and the resolution of the problems in process.

Bachelor's degree or above

At least 3 years of financial work experience

(Technical Skills)
Major in Finance or related fields, proficient in data analysis and project management
Preferred Previous Experiences
Priority given to candidates with work experience in the automotive industry or financial analyst
Behavioral Skills
Good teamwork and logical thinking ability
Special Knowledge
Effective communication skills in English and Mandarin

head: Senior/Market Analyst, Petcoke/Anode
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 重新发布时间: 3 天前 · 17 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Argus is where smart people belong and where they can grow. We answer the challenge of illuminating markets and shaping new futures.
What will you be doing?
We are looking for a driven, passionate, and resourceful
market analyst

to join our team to cover China’s fast-growing petroleum coke and thermal coal markets. The successful candidate will join our global network of over 600 dedicated commodity market analysts, to deliver expert market analyses and information. We pride ourselves on analysing how and why news matter to our high-level audience, which comprises Fortune 500 companies, national oil and energy companies, multinationals, financial institutions, trading companies, and governments.
In this role, you will cover the
China petroleum coke, battery anode materials and other relevant markets

deciding price assessments as well as writing analyses and news stories. You will maintain and expand the contact base, and you will represent Argus at industry events. The analyst will also spearhead development of market coverage alongside their line managers.

You will write engaging market intelligence and provide analysis of spot prices that are timely, accurate, objective, and meaningful for our clients. This includes maintaining a strong network of contacts within the industry to source original, relevant and exclusive content. You will need to be personable and be able to quickly become a subject matter expert and lead coverage within the designated product portfolio. You will enjoy collaborative work with colleagues from various backgrounds, many located in Argus’ other offices around the globe. In addition, there are opportunities to travel, attend industry events, present at industry conferences and lead webinar sessions.

This is an excellent opportunity for market analysts, consultants, or brokers to transfer their skills and knowledge to advance their careers by joining a growing, global, independently owned organisation.
Monitor and assess fast-moving markets to produce daily price assessments, market commentaries, analyses, and reports.
Establish strong relationships with key members of the industry and business leaders through meetings and daily communications to gain an in-depth understanding of your market.

Proactively generate pitch stories, contribute copy to the relevant publication and the online publication service, and contribute to unique content (blog posts, white papers, webinars, podcasts, etc.).
Collaborate with colleagues on the desk and other teams to enhance and drive coverage.
Attend meetings, conferences, and industry events to build contacts and represent Argus in the industry.
Build your industry expertise and writing skills to provide readers with first-class market insights and analysis.
To learn more about the report this role covers, please click on the below link:
Power Generation Industry: Market Insights & Trends | Argus Media
What We’re Looking For In You
A Bachelor’s degree in economics, English, communications, business, statistics or a relevant field.
Prior experience in the financial, energy, commodities or market analysis & consulting sector is beneficial but not essential.
Excellent writing, grammar, and numeracy skills.
Demonstrable interest in global commodity markets.
Strong co-ordination and time management skills
Excellent command of spoken and written English and Chinese.
Strong experience with Microsoft Office Suite with experience in Excel.
A commitment to the highest level of accuracy and completing all writing within established deadlines.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to network and build contacts with industry executives.
Able to work independently while engaging and collaborating in line with the Argus values: Excellence, Integrity, Partnership, and Inclusivity.
What’s In It For You
Work within a highly motivated team with an industry leader and rapidly growing global company.
Competitive salary and bonus structure.
Hybrid working model. Work from Home options available
Career development and progression opportunities.
15 days annual leave.
Group healthcare scheme.
Career development and promotion from within culture.
Ongoing training, mentoring and development opportunities.
Employee Welfare & Employee Assistance Programme.
Travel
Business meetings, cover conferences and events (5-10% of domestic/overseas travel is expected).
Hours
Monday through Friday, 9.00 a.m. to 6.00 p.m.
Earlier or later hours, or weekend hours, may be expected on occasion.

Argus is the leading independent provider of market intelligence to the global energy and commodity markets. We offer essential price assessments, news, analytics, consulting services, data science tools and industry conferences to illuminate complex and opaque commodity markets.
Headquartered in London with 1,500 staff, Argus is an independent media organisation with 30 offices in the world’s principal commodity trading hubs.

Companies, trading firms and governments in 160 countries around the world trust Argus data to make decisions, analyse situations, manage risk, facilitate trading and for long-term planning. Argus prices are used as trusted benchmarks around the world for pricing transportation, commodities and energy.
Founded in 1970, Argus remains a privately held UK-registered company owned by employee shareholders and global growth equity firm General Atlantic.

head: CRM Officer

job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Pizza Hut Hong Kong Management Limited

is the leading pizza restaurant over 120 shops and about 4,700 employees in Hong Kong and Macau. Pizza Hut is committed to be the all-time favorite, serving pizzas anywhere with a delightful touch. To support our mission and expansion, we are now inviting high calibre and passionate professional who strives for excellence to join our team.

Pizza Hut Hong Kong Management Limited is a member of Jardine Restaurant Group Hong Kong, which operates the Pizza Hut, PHD and KFC franchise in Hong Kong and Macau with combined store footprint of over 220 stores and more than 9,000 employees.
What You’ll Do
1. Lead Loyalty Initiatives: Manage and optimize our flagship loyalty program, Hut Rewards, and other marketing campaigns to achieve business goals.
2. Drive Customer Growth: Implement CRM strategies and automation to boost acquisition, retention, and reactivation throughout the customer lifecycle.
3. Turn Data into Action: Analyze member data to design impactful CRM plans and activities that enhance sales and loyalty.
4. Collaborate for Success: Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure flawless execution of campaigns and promotions.
5. Measure Impact: Review campaign performance, track ROI and KPIs, and continuously improve strategies.
6. Innovate & Adapt: Support exciting ad-hoc projects in a fast-paced, test-and-learn environment.
What We’re Looking For
1. Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field.
2. Minimum 3 years of hands-on CRM/Marketing experience, preferably in Retail, F&B, or FMCG.
3. Passionate about F&B and customer engagement.
4. Proactive, detail-oriented, and solution-driven.
5. Strong analytical and negotiation skills with a data-driven mindset.
6. Excellent communication and organizational abilities.
7. Proficient in Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Chinese word processing. Experience with Power BI, CRM platforms, or tools like Braze is a plus.
8. Excellent command of English and Chinese.
9. Candidates with more experience may be considered for Senior CRM Officer.
What we need:
Apply online with your CV and a cover Letter highlighting your experience, skills, and achievements.

Jardine Restaurant Group is responsible for ensuring that all personal information collected from each Candidate presented to Jardine Restaurant Group is used for recruitment purposes only and the data collection process is in accordance with all applicable laws and compliant with the Code of Practice.
To find out more about Our Businesses and Our People, please visit our website https://www.j
rg.com/

Each day, JRG’s 25,000 passionate and diverse employees are committed to sharing the simple joys of food with customers. Throughout the years, JRG has gone from strength to strength through the empowerment of people, innovation, collaboration and sustainability. JRG is a member of the Jardine Matheson Group.

head: Actuarial Analyst
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 3 周前 · 24 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Shape Your Future With Us

General Re Corporation, a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., is a holding company for global reinsurance and related operations, with more than 2,000 employees worldwide. It owns General Reinsurance Corporation and General Reinsurance AG, which conducts business as Gen Re.

Gen Re delivers reinsurance solutions to the Life/Health and Property/Casualty insurance industries. Represented in all major reinsurance markets through a network of 37 offices, we have earned superior financial strength ratings from each of the major rating agencies. We value diversity, equality and inclusion and promote a culture that supports professional and personal growth.
Your Role

Our Corporate Actuarial Service Life/Health Unit is seeking to appoint a dynamic individual as Actuarial Analyst to work in our Shanghai office. In this role, you will support the reserving activities as well as actuarial and financial reporting for Life/Health business in China.
Your Key Responsibilities
1. Support the actuarial reporting under local statutory/IFRS bases and solvency regime
2. Support the reserve calculation (e.g., Mathematical Reserve/IBNR) under German GAAP
3. Support AXIS modelling and maintenance of AXIS model for local statutory (CROSS) and IFRS 17 reporting
4. Support the IFRS 17 implementation in China
5. Conduct, review or validate experience studies to develop reserving bases and best estimate bases
6. Participate in regional and international initiatives and projects
Your Profile
1. A near qualified Actuary with 2 - 5 years of relevant work experience in a life insurance or reinsurance company in China
2. Working knowledge of statutory or local GAAP reporting requirements in China
3. Solid knowledge and prior experience in AXIS or other actuarial software (e.g., Prophet)
4. Proficient in coding (e.g., Python or R), database (e.g.MS Access, SQL) and reporting tools (e.g., Power BI)
5. Good command of Chinese and English, both spoken and written
6. Strong attention to detail and sense of accountability
7. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
8. Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
We offer a
1. highly professional and inclusive work environment that values excellence, integrity, personal responsibility and the unique talent of each employee
2. flat management hierarchy and team-based environment that promote collaboration and free-flowing communication
3. market competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience and qualifications, with a comprehensive suite of employee benefits
Does this sound exciting?
If you want to shape your future with us, we look forward to receiving your application via the Apply Now link.
Our Address
General Reinsurance AG
Shanghai Branch
Unit 021, Level 20, Hang Seng Bank Tower
1000 Lujiazui Ring Road
Shanghai 200120 (People's Republic of China)
company_description: A subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., providing reinsurance solutions globally with over 2,000 employees. It focuses on life/health and property/casualty insurance, offering actuarial services and financial reporting with a commitment to diversity and inclusion.

head: Technical Risk Analyst
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 重新发布时间: 3 天前 · 26 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介

Since its establishment in 2012, our Shanghai office is a rapidly growing participant in the Chinese markets, trading exchange-listed futures, options and equities in China mainland. Our vision is to become the trusted partner in the development of Chinese financial markets. With the culture of a start-up but the backing of a 35+ year-old trading firm, the Optiver Shanghai office is truly unique. Everyone who joins us will help shape the future of our company and its global impact. Get ready: we are only just beginning.
About The Role

As a Technical Risk Analyst within Tech Risk and Enablement (TRE), you will design frameworks that enable risk management at scale and lead initiatives to identify emerging risks across technology, including those introduced by new AI tools and platforms. Partnering with subject matter experts, you will assess control effectiveness and help implement practical solutions that ensure risks are managed effectively and remain within the firm’s appetite.

You’ll play a central role in helping technology functions identify, own, and manage risk effectively, balancing risk and reward to achieve operational excellence while supporting innovation. Acting as a bridge between engineering teams and senior technology leadership (CTOs), you will provide regular risk reporting and insights that inform decision-making. This feedback loop will give you the context to influence teams on the ground and ensure alignment between strategy and execution.

This is a unique opportunity to shape how technology manages risk in APAC, gaining exposure to both strategic leadership and day-to-day engineering operations. Your work will have a visible impact on the resilience, efficiency, and continued growth of Optiver’s technology function.
What You’ll Do
Support technology teams in managing risks in line with our risk appetite.
Provide clear risk reporting and insights to senior management, strengthening decision-making.
Facilitate incident reviews, process deep dives, and cross-team projects to drive improvements.
Surface emerging risks and recurring themes, helping teams address them proactively.
Collaborate with risk and control functions to strengthen frameworks and consistency.
Who You Are
5+ years’ experience in technology risk, IT operations, or operational risk management.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Comfortable engaging with senior leaders while influencing engineering teams.
Pragmatic, collaborative, and resilient in a fast-paced environment.
What you will get
The chance to work alongside diverse and intelligent peers in a rewarding environment.
Competitive remuneration, including an attractive bonus structure
Training, mentorship and personal development opportunities.
Gym membership plus weekly in-house chair massages.
Daily breakfast, lunch and an in-house barista.

As an intentionally flat organisation, we believe that great ideas and impact can come from everyone. We are passionate about empowering individuals and creating diverse teams that thrive. Every person at Optiver should feel included, valued, and respected, because we believe our best work is done together.

Our commitment to diversity and inclusion is hardwired through every stage of our hiring process. We encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds, and we welcome requests for reasonable adjustments during the process to ensure that you can best demonstrate your abilities.
Optiver is committed to
diversity and inclusion
. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds, and welcome requests for reasonable adjustments during the process.

company_box_content: Create better access and prices for all – that’s our goal for financial markets the world over. As a leading global electronic market maker, we are dedicated to our mission of continuously improving the market. It’s a responsibility we don’t take lightly. It’s the driving force behind our business. Even in times of extreme volatility, we stay the course. Resolute in our belief that the market should always be liquid for economies to thrive.

As one of the oldest market-making institutions, we’re a trusted partner of exchanges. Our contribution to them ensures they remain stable, transparent and competitive. We provide liquidity to financial markets using our own capital, at our own risk, trading a wide range of products: listed derivatives, cash equities, ETFs, bonds, and foreign currencies.
We strongly believe in the positive power of the world’s markets. That’s why we’re committed to leading the way in market making for environmental, social, and corporate governance (ESG) products.
Optiver is a global company with offices in Amsterdam, Sydney, Chicago, Austin, Taipei, Hong Kong, Shanghai, London and Singapore.

company_description: A global electronic market maker with offices worldwide, focusing on providing liquidity in financial markets using its own capital. It trades products like derivatives, equities, and currencies, with a commitment to diversity, innovation, and market stability.

info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 重新发布时间: 1 天前 · 56 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介

Wood Mackenzie is the global data and analytics business for the renewables, energy, and natural resources industries. Enhanced by technology. Enriched by human intelligence. In an ever-changing world, companies and governments need reliable and actionable insight to lead the transition to a sustainable future. That’s why we cover the entire supply chain with unparalleled breadth and depth, backed by over 50 years’ experience. Our team of over 2,400 experts, operating across 30 global locations, are enabling customers’ decisions through real-time analytics, consultancy, events and thought leadership. Together, we deliver the insight they need to separate risk from opportunity and make confident decisions when it matters most.
WoodMac.com
Wood Mackenzie Brand Video
Wood Mackenzie Values
Inclusive – we succeed together
Trusting – we choose to trust each other
Customer committed – we put customers at the heart of our decisions
Future Focused – we accelerate change
Curious – we turn knowledge into action
Role Purpose

We monitor key events and conduct research to produce thought-provoking reports, data models, and presentations. From asset valuation to corporate reports, from market supply-demand research to price-forecasting, and from the regional to the global, our analysis helps shape our customers’ commercial strategies.

We also support bespoke consulting projects, diving deeper into our data to answer unique questions. We help our Sales team retain and win new business by spending time with customers and answering questions that matter to them. We work closely with colleagues in our Data and Product functions to maintain the quality of our published data and to deliver content via our
growing Lens platform

Through carrying out primary research, responding to customer questions, and presenting to industry stakeholders, you will build relationships and a network of contacts at key operators, national bodies, and other relevant institutions. Over time you will gain specialist knowledge and develop the skills you require to progress your career at Wood Mackenzie.
Main Responsibilities

We are looking for an Analyst to work as part of our Research team covering Metals & Mining. We are particularly interested in hearing from people with experience in any of the following commodities – nickel, graphite, battery raw materials, or rare earth minerals.

You will have relevant experience, skills or knowledge that you can apply to the role, allowing you to contribute to our research from day one. Your existing knowledge will enhance our coverage as you develop your career.

Diversity is at the core of our business, and we welcome applications from a range of academic or professional backgrounds. Wood Mackenzie has adopted a hybrid approach, and we expect people to be present in our offices 2 days per week.
Your application should showcase your enthusiasm for the sectors we cover and demonstrate your ability in the following areas, which we require daily at Wood Mackenzie.

Research and data gathering – we identify and gather intelligence that feeds our industry expertise. We need inquisitive and enthusiastic people with a keen eye for detail. Conduct detailed research and analysis of graphite mining operations globally. Author regular publications providing our near-term and long-term outlook, focusing on pricing and demand analysis.

Analysis – we provide market-leading research using this data/intelligence and form opinions for and tell stories to our customers. We need people with natural curiosity with the potential to become experts in their field and with the ability to articulate – both verbally and in writing – clear, insightful analysis. Maintain and enhance proprietary data models and methodologies that underpin our graphite market analysis. Monitor market developments, regulatory changes, and technological trends impacting the industry.

Internal engagement – we collaborate with colleagues across the Wood Mackenzie business to deliver a compelling, integrated view of the commodities sector to customers through our innovative products. For this, we need people who are as comfortable working collaboratively as they are independently and who have excellent people skills.

External engagement – we proactively connect with our customers and contacts in the industries we serve, adding value and promoting the Wood Mackenzie brand. This requires people with the potential to become industry experts who have intellectual curiosity, passion, and excellent presentation skills to communicate with clients. Develop and maintain contacts with industry stakeholders to gather intelligence that will enrich the quality and timeliness of our research solution

Leadership/ Process Improvement – at Wood Mackenzie, we are committed to transforming the way we power our planet. The energy transition is here, and this impacts our customer base and how we serve them. We need colleagues with a change mindset, who are flexible, growth-orientated, and initiative-taking.
Preferred Skills & Experience
A degree in mining engineering, geology, economics, metallurgical engineering, or a related discipline.

2–6 years of relevant experience in the mining industry, consulting, or market analysis, ideally with exposure to any of the following commodities – nickel, graphite, battery raw materials or rare earth minerals.
Strong quantitative and qualitative research skills and good working knowledge of PowerPoint.
Excellent numerical analytic capabilities, including problem-solving and technical skills in Microsoft Excel.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
additional languages are a plus.
The ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Commitment to commercial excellence and experience managing clients.
A proactive and adaptable mindset, with a willingness to contribute to a variety of tasks and projects.
Expectations
Growth mindset - taking the initiative to learn new skills, seeking out feedback and actively applying what has been learned in the workplace.
Change agility - maintaining high performance through disruption and change.

We are a hybrid working company and the successful applicant will be expected to be physically present in the office at least 2 days per week to foster and contribute to a collaborative environment. This is subject to change per company policy.
This is a full-time role.
Diversity is at the core of our business
and we welcome applications from a range of academic or professional backgrounds.

head: Analyst, Aluminium Assets
info: 中国 北京市 · 2 周前 · 31 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
About CRU:

CRU International is a leading provider of business intelligence and consulting services in the metals, mining, and fertilizer industries. With over 50 years of experience, we offer valuable insights and analysis that help our clients make informed decisions in an ever-changing global market.
About the role:

CRU seeks an Aluminium Assets Analyst to deliver asset-focused subscription services and convert complex datasets into clear, client-ready insight. The role will maintain aluminium asset models and datasets, conduct primary and secondary research with industry contacts, and translate findings into market-shaping analysis and timely service deliverables. The analyst will support Service Leads, collaborate across the Assets and broader analysis teams to ensure consistent, high-quality delivery, and help identify commercial opportunities to grow the service.
Key Responsibilities:
1. Build and maintain a strong understanding of aluminium commodity markets and the assets within remit
2. Contribute to asset-level analysis and modelling for the Service(s) under remit
3. Maintain, organise and update the data series and aluminium asset models that form part of customer deliverables
4. Conduct primary and secondary research, including developing and managing relationships with market, industry and institutional contacts
5. Translate research and datasets into clear, market-shaping analysis and timely client deliverables
6. Attend industry conferences and events to build networks and represent CRU
7. Support client-facing activities including meetings, demos and webinars
8. Respond to internal requests for data and asset-related information and help identify commercial opportunities to grow the service
Qualifications:
1. Essential: Bachelor’s degree from a top-tier institution
2. Preferred: Further education or certification in a subject relevant to commodity research (e.g., geology, economics, engineering)
Skills & Experience:
1. Strong ability to gather and critically analyse both “soft” (qualitative) and “hard” (quantitative) data to identify trends and drivers and generate actionable insights
2. Highly numerate with experience interpreting, manipulating and managing large datasets and understanding relevant mathematical concepts and processes
3. Educational background in a quantitative subject
4. Experience conducting primary and secondary research and engaging with industry contacts
5. Able to collaborate across global analysis teams, share best practice, and balance personal initiative with team-based working
6. Strong verbal and written communication skills for client deliverables and presentations
What We Offer:
1. Competitive salary and flexible benefits package.
2. Opportunities for professional growth and development as part of a global company.
3. A collaborative and supportive work environment.
4. The chance to work with industry-leading experts and over a diverse range of topics and projects.
company_box_content: Commodity markets are more dynamic and complex than ever, shaped by volatility, new regulations, and rapid technological change. At CRU, we empower our clients to navigate this landscape with clarity and confidence—delivering trusted, independent data and insights with speed and precision.

Since 1969, CRU has been a global leader in business intelligence for the metals, mining, and fertilizer industries. Our expertise spans market analysis, commodity data and price assessments, strategic consultancy, and vibrant industry communities and events. With over 450 experts worldwide—including teams on the ground in China—we combine robust primary research with deep local knowledge to help you stay ahead in fast-moving markets.

Our acquisition of Exawatt strengthens our capabilities in electrification technologies, from renewable power generation to solar PV and energy storage. As industries accelerate their drive to decarbonise, CRU delivers meaningful data and insight to support your sustainability journey.

We’ve unified our brand to make it simpler and more intuitive to engage with CRU—whether you’re accessing our data platforms, joining our community events, or partnering with our consulting teams. In a world of constant change, CRU is committed to propelling your success forward—deciphering market trends, shaping sustainable strategies, and empowering you every step of the way.
Follow CRU Group on Twitter: @crugroup

Amazon Global Selling has been helping individuals and businesses increase sales and reach new customers around the globe. Today, more than 50% of Amazon's total unit sales come from third-party selection. The Global Selling team is responsible for recruiting local businesses to sell on Amazon’s 19+ overseas marketplaces and supporting local Sellers’ success and growth on the Amazon. Our vision is to be the first choice for all types of business to go globally.

In our effort to support this business growth around the world, we are looking for a passionate Sr. Business Intelligence Engineer to turn data into actionable insights, and support business operation and strategic decision making via business intelligence solution and artificial intelligence tools. This role will collaborate with cross-functional teams and multiple business time to design and develop data solution and enhance analytics capabilities for BI and AI Automation initiatives.
Key job responsibilities
Translating business questions and concerns into specific analytical questions that can be answered with available data using BI tools
produce the required data when it is not available.
Apply Statistical and Machine Learning methods to specific business problems and data.

Ensure data quality throughout all stages of acquisition and processing, including such areas as data sourcing/collection, ground truth generation, normalization, transformation, cross-lingual alignment/mapping, etc.
Communicate proposals and results in a clear manner backed by data and coupled with actionable conclusions to drive business decisions.
Collaborate with colleagues from multidisciplinary science, engineering and business backgrounds.
Develop efficient data querying and modeling infrastructure.
Manage your own process. Prioritize and execute on high impact projects, triage external requests, and ensure to deliver projects in time.
Utilizing code (SQL, Python, R, etc.) for analyzing data and building statistical models.

Master's degree in BI, Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics or related field.
5+ years of professional experience in business intelligence, data science or analytics position.
Advanced working knowledge of data engineering, ETL pipeline, and data modeling.
Demonstrated experience data querying languages (e.g. SQL), scripting languages (e.g. Python) or statistical/mathematical software (e.g. R, SAS, Matlab, etc.) experience
